title: Friedrichsbrücke - Quick Summary
items: **Friedrichsbrücke is the quiet bridge between Museum Island and central Mitte,** connecting Bodestraße with the area toward Hackescher Markt.
**Its story starts in 1703,** when a wooden bridge called the Große Pomeranzenbrücke crossed the Spree here.
**The bridge was renamed for Frederick the Great around 1790-1792,** after earlier 18th-century rebuilding and changes to the road surface.
**The most dramatic version came in 1893-1894,** with a wider three-arch bridge, obelisks and decorative lighting figures.
**The bridge you cross today is a rebuilt pedestrian bridge,** shaped by 1945 destruction, a 1982 reconstruction and a 2012-2014 heritage-sensitive widening.
